items.headline: Anti-trafficking group says CLARITY Act's Section 604 could weaken accountability
items.subheadline: An anti-human trafficking advocate said a Clarity Act provision could weaken accountability despite existing criminal laws.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: U.S. House Democrat, who may soon run key committee, condemns crypto in 401(k)s
items.subheadline: Maxine Waters, the ranking Democrat on House Financial Services, asked the chief of the Department of Labor to withdraw its proposal on alternative assets.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Crypto’s security nightmare won’t be solved by ordinary audits
items.subheadline: Without an update to the current auditing infrastructure, the crypto space will likely continue to suffer significant losses, explains Beyer.
items.author: Stefan Beyer

items.headline: The SEC delayed tokenizing stocks, and here’s why that’s a relief
items.subheadline: Before we rush to tokenize U.S. equities, we need compliance systems that understand context, not just checkboxes. Investors like Michael Burry see the danger, so should the rest of us, urges Chamarajnagar.
items.author: Dr. Ravishankar Chamarajnagar

items.headline: The banking lobby is wrong about stablecoins and community banks
items.subheadline: Congress should not kneecap one of the clearest advances in payment infrastructure to protect community banks from a threat that has not been proven, argues Eco CEO Ryne Saxe.
items.author: Ryne Sax

items.headline: What Robinhood’s recent layoffs say about the current state of crypto investments
items.subheadline: Despite the trading platform’s restructuring and the wave of crypto companies making cuts, signaling a late bear market, Altcoin Pro’s Horst, Anderson and Zhuleku explain there’s no reason to panic.
items.author: Ryan Horst

items.headline: Aave, Solana lead crypto price gains as bitcoin (BTC) steadies near $60,000
items.subheadline: Tokenized stock trading fueled fresh momentum across the Solana ecosystem, while Aave founder hinted at token buybacks coming under new framework.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: ETH, XRP, DOGE price news: Majors fall 9% over week as AI stocks lure buyers
items.subheadline: A rotation out of chipmakers lifted much of the stock market, with the equal-weight S&P 500 at a record. Crypto was not part of it. Ether fell 8% on the week and the memecoins dropped harder.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ripple CEO stays bullish on bitcoin but says Saylor's strategy has hurt crypto
items.subheadline: Ripple's Brad Garlinghouse called Strategy's preferred-stock funding model "financial engineering" that distracted the market, pointing to STRC's slide to a record low as the evidence. He runs the company behind XRP, a bitcoin rival.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live markets: Bitcoin falls below $60,000. Kospi, Nikkei sink
items.subheadline: BTC sees a relief bounce as Asian stocks wilt following sharp losses on Wall Street.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: How to better understand bitcoin’s perpetual identity crisis
items.subheadline: Here, Bailey unpacks the uncertainty surrounding the original cryptocurrency’s nature, which creates inconsistent market behavior.
items.author: Steve Bailey

items.headline: Live updates: Strategy buys 520 more bitcoin and raises reserves to $1.4 billion
items.subheadline: Eased ETF selling and improving risk appetite are being offset by a firmer dollar and cautious institutional flows, leaving bitcoin range-bound, one analyst said.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Former Ethereum Foundation leader warns of funding gap as governance shifts
items.subheadline: A former Ethereum Foundation member says the network must quickly build new funding institutions as the Foundation steps back.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: a16z-backed crypto firm rebrands, shifts focus to solving AI’s global copyright headache
items.subheadline: The startup formerly known as Story Protocol raised $140 million to secure internet rights and is now building an audit layer for data consent, licensing, and provenance for tech firms.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Kraken eyes 15% stake in DeFi lender Aave in deal valuing protocol at $385 million
items.subheadline: The DeFi lender is rebuilding after the fallout from April's KelpDAO exploit sparked a multibillion-dollar exodus of deposits despite Aave itself not being hacked.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Invesco, $2.5T asset manager, files for tokenized fund targeting stablecoin reserves
items.subheadline: The $2.5 trillion asset manager deepens its blockchain push after taking over Superstate's tokenized money market fund as fund manager earlier this year.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: BTC price news: Grant Cardone will keep buying bitcoin using real estate cash flows
items.subheadline: The real estate investor pitched his model as a treasury company backed by cash-flowing property rather than stock sales, framing the slide in bitcoin as a chance to accumulate.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Japanese giant SBI Holdings to buy Bitbank for $289 million
items.subheadline: SBI said the acquisition, which is subject to regulatory approval, is set to close in October.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Surging U.S. IPO market still falls short of bubble territory: Goldman Sachs
items.subheadline: U.S. IPO issuance has rebounded sharply in 2026, but the bank said the current surge lacks the deal volume and speculative excess that defined the dot-com era.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: BlackRock-backed Securitize to raise $400 million nearing public debut; CEPT jumps 8%
items.subheadline: The BlackRock-backed tokenization specialist expects to close its SPAC merger next week and start trading on the NYSE, pending shareholder approval.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Taiko halts Ethereum layer-2 network after a bridge exploit, token dives
items.subheadline: An attacker forged withdrawal proofs to drain about $1.7 million, the same type of flaw behind this year's biggest bridge hacks. Fast containment kept the damage small.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: talent exodus sparks fresh debate over foundation leadership
items.subheadline: The Ethereum community is once again debating the future of the foundation after co-executive director Hsiao-Wei Wang announced she would step down from her leadership role, adding to a growing list of departures that have intensified scrutiny of the organization's management and direction.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Quantum computing is often seen as a risk to bitcoin. Now Trump wants to develop it.
items.subheadline: The White House issued twin executive orders to accelerate U.S. development of large-scale quantum computers while simultaneously hardening defenses against such machines.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Foundation cuts 20% of staff amid leadership exodus
items.subheadline: The reduction follows a period of significant upheaval at the organization’s leadership level.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will cut budget 40% in major reset
items.subheadline: The reduction comes on the same day the EF confirmed a 20% reduction in headcount and follows the resignation of co-Executive Director Hsiao-Wei Wang. Her departure brings the total number of senior Ethereum Foundation figures to leave since January to nine, underscoring the scale of the organization's ongoing turmoil.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin claws back some of the losses after Micron beats earnings
items.subheadline: Battered crypto sector gets some relief after Micron beats earnings, bringing back some risk appetite to the market.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Upheaval at the foundation has some of crypto’s biggest names feeling bullish
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we're looking at Ethereum's eventful week that started off with the launch of EthLabs, plus the layoffs at the Ethereum Foundation, and what this all means for the network.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Live markets: Bitcoin, ether lead $1 billion liquidation losses as AI trade keeps going
items.subheadline: Strategy's common stock hit a multi-year low, while its STRC preferred stock plunged to a new record low.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Coinbase (COIN) news: Base blockchain resumes after two-hour outage disrupted network
items.subheadline: The incident temporarily halted transaction processing on one of Ethereum's largest layer-2 networks.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Why tokenization is an ETF-style market structure revolution
items.subheadline: The current tokenization dialogue and pattern resemble ETFs’ early days, which ultimately transformed into a $10+ trillion market, Lie argues.
items.author: Michael Lie

items.headline: Meta is paying creators in Stablecoins. Spending them is someone else's problem
items.subheadline: Meta’s decision to pay creators in USDC validates stablecoins as a mainstream disbursement tool, Joslyn suggests, but it also exposes the industry’s unresolved problem: moving seamlessly from digital dollars to usable local currency.
items.author: Tim Joslyn

items.headline: 5 corruption gaps Congress must close in the Clarity Act
items.subheadline: The most consequential crypto legislation in the world is moving forward in the U.S. Senate. As written, it leaves the United States exposed to money laundering, sanctions evasion, and conflicts of interest at the highest levels of government, argues Greytak.
items.author: Scott Greytak

items.headline: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
items.subheadline: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.
items.author: Samir Tabar

items.headline: The U.S. government is betting $2 Billion on quantum computing, and the defense side can't keep up
items.subheadline: Pruden argues that to defend against a quantum computer capable of cryptographically relevant operations, we need post-quantum cryptography and regulatory coordination that the industry has been deferring for years.
items.author: Alex  Pruden

items.headline: Stablecoins Were Meant to Disrupt Finance. Instead, They Became Idle Cash.
items.subheadline: O’Connor argues that crypto’s clearest success story has scaled as money but not as capital.
items.author: John O’Connor

items.headline: If America wants to lead in crypto, it must protect the people who build it
items.subheadline: Despite the Clarity Act’s advancement toward the finish line, there’s one provision under threat for builders that can’t be overlooked, argues Smith.
items.author: Kristin Smith

items.headline: CZ, Binance founder, wants to clear up 'misunderstandings' about who he is
items.subheadline: The former CEO of the world's largest crypto exchange is seeking to redefine himself to the world on his own terms.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Ex-FCA policy insider explains the ‘great divide’ in the UK’s crypto ambition
items.subheadline: Former FCA policymaker and Hedera Global Policy VP, Isadora Arredondo says there is a gap between the U.K.'s crypto ambitions and how policy is carried out in practice.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Trump refuses to sign law with U.S. CBDC ban, demands approval of elections bill
items.subheadline: As Congress prepared to celebrate the president's signing of the bipartisan housing bill that contains a CBDC prohibition, Trump abruptly cancelled the event.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Binance withdraws Greek MiCA bid but vows to remain in the EU
items.subheadline: The crypto giant must find a home base in the EU by July 1 or regulators will force the company to shut down operations for millions of regional users.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: CoinEx denies claims it served as $3.84 billion gateway to sanctioned Iranian crypto firms
items.subheadline: Blockchain analysts TRM Labs says CoinEx facilitated more than $3.8 billion in blockchain-traced flows with sanctioned Iranian crypto entities. CoinEx disputes the findings.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Post-prison CZ says time behind bars didn't hurt the billionaire's business after Binance
items.subheadline: The pardoned ex-CEO called prison a character-building "plus" as he's pursued a venture-capital career, despite his majority stake in the world's biggest crypto exchange.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Kraken sues crypto derivatives firm PowerTrade over missing funds
items.subheadline: PowerTrade changed Kraken’s positive balance of over $6 milllion to a $2 million deficit via a series of unauthorized “corrections” related to trades that had expired or settled months earlier, according to a legal filing.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Binance tells EU users it will no longer provide services after failing to secure MiCA license
items.subheadline: A day after withdrawing its MiCA license application in Greece and saying it is ‘not leaving Europe,’ Binance notified users across the European Union that it will suspend services.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: MSTR's BTC premium has vanished as enterprise mNAV falls below 1
items.subheadline: For years, investors had valued the firm well above its bitcoin holdings, giving Strategy massive flexibility to raise capital as needed — a situation Michael Saylor and team took full advantage of.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Binance founder CZ blames crypto's sour 2026 on mix of AI, global tension, 4-year cycle
items.subheadline: There is no single cause for the crypto market's 50% decline over the past year, the Binance founder told CoinDesk.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Coinbase and OKX try to lure in Binance’s users after it failed to secure a MiCA license
items.subheadline: Brian Armstrong of Coinbase and Star Xu of OKX offered new users sign up bonuses of up to 8% of their deposits or transfers from other accounts.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Polymarket hack updated to $3.1 million days after the platform promised users full refunds
items.subheadline: The prediction markets giant is under investigation in connection with false or deceptive marketing practices, according to news reports.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Tether puts $23 billion gold stockpile to work
items.subheadline: Tether and Ledn are launching support for XAU₮, allowing users to borrow against tokenized gold as the stablecoin issuer expands the utility of its $23 billion bullion holdings.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: BTC price analysis: Why a selloff in gold and silver is dragging bitcoin down
items.subheadline: Bitcoin has long been lumped in with precious metals as a hedge against a weakening dollar. That trade is unwinding on a hawkish Fed, and bitcoin is falling alongside the metals it was supposed to rival.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: BTC price news: Bitcoin under $60,000 on track for a rare back-to-back quarterly loss
items.subheadline: The token is down nearly 7% on the week with the altcoins falling harder. Bitcoin and ether are both ending the second quarter in the red, a back-to-back losing first half that runs against the usual pattern.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Tokenization is becoming the financing layer for AI and robotics, Framework bets with $400 million fund
items.subheadline: Blockchain is becoming the financial layer for capital-intensive industries rather than just crypto-native speculation, Michael Anderson, the crypto venture firm's cofounder said.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Why SBI paid $289 million for an unprofitable crypto exchange: Architect Partners
items.subheadline: The investment bank said the purchase of Japanese crypto exchange Bitbank is a bet on regulated scale as sweeping reforms reshape the country's digital asset market.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Michael Saylor teases more bitcoin buying even as Strategy stock continues to fall
items.subheadline: The crypto bull shrugged off market skepticism by sharing a chart of the company's $50 billion stash and teasing that they are going to need more data points.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Samson Mow says bitcoin bottom is in, but analysts remain divided
items.subheadline: The bitcoin advocate said his crypto bottom claim is based on the fact that the traditional four-year halving cycle has changed, although several analysts continue to expect further downside.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: CZ wants to make the U.S. the 'capital of crypto': State of Crypto
items.subheadline: The Binance founder shared his views on a variety of topics in an exclusive interview with CoinDesk earlier this month.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: $4 billion gone. Spot bitcoin ETFs are on track for their worst month on record
items.subheadline: Investors have pulled out $4 billion from the U.S.-listed spot bitcoin ETFs in June, the highest outflow on record.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Live BTC markets: Bitcoin dips to $59,700 as Iran de-escalation lifts stocks
items.subheadline: U.S. equity futures rose after reports the U.S. and Iran agreed to halt strikes and resume talks. Bitcoin has barely moved, still down 6.8% on the week.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin bottom might not be in as S.Korea announces massive $518 billion AI chip push
items.subheadline: Samsung and SK Hynix are pulling a chip-plant buildout forward by a decade to meet AI memory demand. It is the latest and largest sign of the AI capital cycle that has drawn money away from crypto all year.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Crypto exchange BitMEX removes CEO, CFO and head of growth
items.subheadline: The firm’s global general counsel, Peter Wilkinson, has taken over as CEO from Stephan Lutz.
items.author: Ian Allison
